items.title: 'How many beers have you had?': Flood evacuees claim racial profiling

children.content: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

gallery.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

thumbnail.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

items.title: Paramedics unable to help bleeding mother for almost an hour, inquest hears

children.content: A paramedic tells an inquest she decided to wait for police back-up before attending to a woman who was actively harming herself in a Northern Territory domestic violence shelter.

image.altText: The exterior of the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: Police allege trio used edged weapons, slingshot in 'violent incident'

children.content: A man remains in a critical condition in hospital, and three others have been charged by police, after a "violent" altercation in the remote Northern Territory community of Ngukurr.

image.altText: An aerial photo of houses in a remote community

items.title: Remote residents 'cooking from the inside' sue NT government landlord

children.content: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say the scorching hot outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
A woman in a brightly coloured short with grey hair sits in a darkened room holding a white metal cup.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.

items.title: China suddenly grants new beef licences to Australia

children.content: China has granted beef export approval to several new Australian facilities, just months after introducing a range of trade measures to protect its domestic cattle industry.

image.altText: Chef cooking beef with China and Australian flags in the background

items.title: Why this man risked crocodiles, storms and jail to fish in Australian waters

children.content: Why do Indonesian crews keep fishing illegally in Australian waters when the activity is so perilous?

items.title: Iran's meme factory is using 'the weapons of their enemy' to embarrass Trump

children.content: AI-generated Lego memes criticising the conflict in the Middle East and mocking Donald Trump are attracting a large audience online as a new front opens in the Iran war.

items.title: Mark Butler announces major NDIS overhaul — as it happened

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler says the scheme was designed to support 410,000 people with a disability, but currently supports 760,000.

items.title: Staff face misconduct proceedings after foster kids allowed to live with killer

children.content: Some Department of Communities and Justice staff members will face misconduct proceedings after a bungle that saw two foster children living with triple killer Regina Arthurell earlier this year.

image.altText: A selfie picture of a person in blonde hard and big dark sunglasses

items.title: Closure of specialist centres for children with autism puts spotlight on NDIS funding

children.content: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

articles.title: 500,000 Australians wait for end to 'limbo' as Butler readies NDIS plan

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

image.altText: Two men in dark suits enter the lower house.

items.title: The artworks we can't stop thinking about from the Adelaide Biennial

children.content: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

items.title: Woman's life 'changed for the better' after GPs in SA trained to diagnose ADHD

children.content: About 100 South Australian GPs are now qualified to diagnose ADHD and provide treatment, with the hope of that number doubling by the end of the year, with one patient describing her diagnosis through her GP as "life-changing".

image.altText: A person standing in the Adelaide CBD.

items.title: Uncounted SA votes in the 'order of dozens', Attorney-General says

children.content: The number of newly revealed uncounted ballot papers from the March state election is believed to be in the "order of dozens", South Australia's Attorney-General says.

image.altText: A person wearing a blue vest with the words Electoral Officer on it among people lining up at a polling booth

items.title: 'Terrifying' experience for WA family as storm tears roof off their home

image.altText: Letterbox reading '10' in the foreground with a gutted home in the background.

items.title: Chocolate-box violin inspires teen to retrace relative's Anzac footsteps

children.content: Ernest Sidney Pilcher died of his wounds in France on June 25, 1918. This Anzac Day, inspired by a remarkable family relic, his great-great-great-nephew will deliver a eulogy at his gravesite.

gallery.altText: A mid shot of a teenage boy holding a rectangular violin made from a wooden chocolate box in WW1
A black and white image of a group of WWI soldiers. One at the back holds a wooden violin.

items.title: Three weeks' pay guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers

items.title: Why aspiring home owners cannot move into brand new homes

children.content: Five years after signing contracts and paying their deposits, families say their lives have been put on hold as they wait to move into their "forever homes" in Newcastle.

image.altText: Two females, and a male, standing outside a property with their hands clasping a makeshift construction fence.

items.title: Carpooling an 'effective solution' to fuel prices, so why aren't more doing it?

children.content: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

items.title: Rates bill shock forecast as councils struggle with debt

items.title: Eczema medication costing families thousands due to PBS age limit

children.content: The cost of relief for kids under 12 with severe eczema is $1,600 a month, but a push for PBS funding is looking to change that.

items.title: Meet the eco-venture turning an invasive pest into dog treats

children.content: Introducing the European carp to Australian waterways is an ecological disaster that continues to worsen, but an Adelaide couple have found a novel use for the pest.

image.altText: Young woman feeds a kelpie a treat

items.title: Young benches ex-coach's son and fires assistant to start Dragons tenure

children.content: Dean Young names the first team of his tenure as Dragons coach, dropping Kyle Flanagan to the bench and sacking assistant and attack coach Michael Ennis.
